pytorch_task_v1.prompt_instructions: Implement this ML task as a SINGLE self-contained Python file (task.py). Include all required functions AND a complete `if __name__ == '__main__':` block that trains the model, evaluates on a validation split, prints standard metrics (MSE, R2, parameter accuracy where applicable), and asserts quality thresholds so the script exits non-zero on failure. Do NOT create separate test files or README. The script itself is the test.

tasks.description: Univariate linear regression on a noisy synthetic dataset using mini-batch SGD with a learning-rate scheduler and gradient clipping.

requirements.data: Synthetic: y = -1.5x + 0.7 + noise; 1000 samples, 80/20 train/validation split.
requirements.implementation: Use torch.utils.data.DataLoader for mini-batches; implement SGD with optional momentum, gradient clipping, and StepLR scheduler.
requirements.evaluation: The evaluate() function must compute and return MSE and R2 on both train and validation splits; report learned parameters vs true coefficients.
requirements.main_block: The main block must train long enough for good convergence, print metrics and parameters, and assert R2_val > 0.9 and parameter L2 error < 0.5.
requirements.output: Return dict with loss_history, val_loss_history, and final train/val metrics.

tasks.description: Binary logistic regression on a 2D Gaussian dataset with feature noise augmentation and L2 weight decay.

requirements.data: Two 2D Gaussian clusters with some overlap; 1000 samples, standardized features, 80/20 train/validation split.
requirements.implementation: Use torch.nn for a linear classifier and BCEWithLogitsLoss with weight decay; apply Gaussian noise augmentation to inputs during training.
requirements.evaluation: The evaluate() function must compute accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 on the validation split.
requirements.main_block: The main block must train the model, print metrics, and assert validation accuracy > 0.9 and F1 > 0.9.
requirements.output: Return dict with train/val loss history and classification metrics.

tasks.description: Train a small CNN on a synthetic MNIST-like dataset generated from random blobs with data augmentation and early stopping.

requirements.data: Synthetic 1-channel 16x16 images with 3 classes generated from Gaussian blobs; 2000 samples, 80/20 train/validation split.
requirements.implementation: Use nn.Conv2d-based CNN with ReLU and max-pooling; apply random flips and small affine transforms for augmentation; use Adam optimizer.
requirements.evaluation: The evaluate() function must compute accuracy and macro-F1 on the validation split.
requirements.main_block: Include early stopping on validation accuracy, print best epoch metrics, and assert validation accuracy > 0.9.
requirements.output: Return dict with history of train/val loss and accuracy, and final best metrics.

tasks.description: Sequence-to-one regression on a synthetic noisy sine-wave time series using a GRU-based model.

requirements.data: Generate a univariate sine-wave with noise; build sliding windows of length 20 and predict the next value; 80/20 train/validation split.
requirements.implementation: Use nn.GRU followed by a linear head; train with MSE loss and Adam optimizer; support GPU if available.
requirements.evaluation: The evaluate() function must compute MSE and R2 on train and validation splits.
requirements.main_block: Train until convergence with a patience-based early stopping, print metrics, and assert validation R2 > 0.9 and MSE < 0.05.
requirements.output: Return dict with train/val loss history and final metrics.
